skoda duobell

the first bike bell designed to penetrate noise cancelling headphones

skoda found the one frequency ANC can't fully block (750hz) and built a mechanical bell around it with a second frequency for extra reliability. tested in london, heard 22 meters earlier than a standard bell. analog solution to a digital problem

Claude Mythos Preview by @AnthropicAI

anthropic released opus 4.6 just 2 months ago. now they have a new model that found thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities in every major OS and browser. some of these bugs survived decades of human review.

they locked it in a sandbox. it escaped, got to the open internet, then emailed the researcher to brag about it.

10 partners including apple, google, microsoft, and nvidia are now using it to secure critical software. $100M in credits. confirmed it wont be released to the public.

AI is now better than most humans at breaking software. defenders need to move fast.

https://www.anthropic.com/glasswing

#ai #security #anthropic

dogfood by chris tate

an agent-browser skill that uses your app like a real user.

- point it at any url
- explores, clicks, and fills forms
- tests edge cases + console checks
- captures repro videos & screenshots
- structured reports with severity ratings

no scripts. no manual qa.

https://skills.sh/vercel-labs/agent-browser/dogfood

SpellUI by 16 year old Tom

another clean ui component library

https://spell.sh

CultUI by nollystudios

really clean shadcn-compatible component library with a lottt of really good/useful components. check it out

https://www.cult-ui.com

hit-area by bazza

tailwind classes to fix interaction dead zones. uses pseudo-elements to expand hit areas without messing up your layout.

finally a practical way to ship this across a whole codebase.

https://bazza.dev/craft/2026/hit-area
npx shadcn@latest add https://bazza.dev/r/hit-area

cobe by shuding

stunning 5kb webgl globe library for landing pages. ultra-light, customizable, and smooth as hell.

https://cobe.vercel.app/
npm i cobe

someone just open sourced a full 3d building editor that runs in the browser. no autocad, no revit, no $5k licenses.

it’s called pascal editor. built with react three fiber + webgpu for near-native speed.

features:
- real-time building/wall hierarchy
- gpu-powered ecs architecture
- zustand state (undo/redo)
- next.js frontend
- dirty node tracking for fast renders

you can stack, explode, or solo levels and edit everything in 3d. firms pay $50k+ for bim software that does this.

this is 100% open source and free.

https://editor.pascal.app/
https://github.com/pascalorg/editor
LOVEE this artstyle. closest i could find to a web-based editor for this was https://thebicycle42.com with dithering and half dots
